last_result.effect: This asks the manager for information only; it does not change the EasyCrypt proof state.
last_result.result: The manager returned read-only context for the current goal.

goal_info.remaining_goals_note: You have 4 subgoals pending. The `goal_type` above is for the CURRENT (first pending) goal ONLY — the other 3 subgoal(s) may have DIFFERENT types. This is normal after tactics like `call (_: Inv)`, `byequiv`, `conseq`, or `seq K:` which each generate multiple subgoals with mixed pRHL/phoare/hoare/ambient shapes. Do NOT interpret a mismatch between the current-goal type and your proof plan as 'session is broken'. Close the current subgoal, then inspect the next one via `-goal-info` again. Use `-status` to see total proof progress.

remaining_goals_inference.description: Pending subgoal from a branching tactic earlier in history; this entry isn't from the latest `case`. Runmanager on each subgoal as you reach it to see its actual shape.
Pending subgoal from a branching tactic earlier in history; this entry isn't from the latest `case`. Runmanager on each subgoal as you reach it to see its actual shape.

goal_info.remaining_goals_inference_caveat: These subgoal shapes are INFERRED from the last branching tactic's known pattern, NOT read from EC directly (EC's emacs output only exposes the current goal). Use as hints, not ground truth. Ground truth is obtained by closing the current subgoal and runningmanager on the next. Known-wrong cases: 3-arg `call (_: bad, Inv)`, nested branchers, while-variants with different arg counts.

lines: &hr: {b : bool, i, j : int, s, s0 : int list, r, r' : word}
big predT<:int> a PIR.s{hr} +^ big predT<:int> a PIR.s'{hr} =
if i0 < j{hr} then a i0 else zerow) /\
if i0 < j{hr} + 1 then a i0 else zerow) /\

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

moves.title: Residual closing tactic
moves.applicability: This tactic-shaped option should be checked with a read-only probe before committing it.
moves.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
moves.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from proof-state analysis
moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

moves.title: Read-only tactic probe
moves.applicability: This tactic-shaped option should be checked with a read-only probe before committing it.

moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

obligation_families.effect: The remaining membership obligation relates an updated map key to a list or set membership fact.
obligation_families.limitations: The surface reports the alignment facts currently visible; it does not synthesize missing equalities.

obligation_families.effect: The current proof point is dominated by local logical obligations rather than program-frontier movement.
obligation_families.limitations: Quantifier names are not normalized across EasyCrypt pretty-printer variants.

visible_destructors.effect: Splits list membership into head and tail cases.
visible_destructors.limitations: The surface does not decide which case applies.

membership_decomposition_surface.effect: Summarizes list/map/filter membership sources whose local case facts are visible in the current pure tail.
limitations: No destructor tactic is chosen by this surface.
The panel does not assume which membership branch is true.

pure_tail_surface.effect: Summarizes verifier-visible pure-goal structure after program frontier actions have been discharged or hidden by local proof steps.
limitations: This panel records syntactic and proof-state facts from the current view.
It does not choose a tactic or instantiate a lemma.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: latest tactic event goals_after differs from current EC state (3 != 4)
proof-state analysis sees no live call sites after a prior `inline *` at tactic 3. Continue at procedure/ambient layer, or undo if a call lemma is needed.
Some imported or cross-scope handles may need lookup if that route is being tested; use lookup_candidates for the concrete symbol instead of treating the whole list as a to-do queue.

evidence: pure_tail_surface is visible for the current goal
current proof state has no program-frontier work before the logical residual
limitations: does not select a destructor, witness, rewrite, or SMT lemma
reported checkpoints are recovery references, not a default route

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Need local or standard-library lemma names before rewriting a pure goal.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and qualified-name lookup guidance

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid EasyCrypt form for rewrite, apply, conseq, while, or SMT setup.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

items.label: Before inline expansion #3

items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #3 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
